name: Acoramidis improved ATTR-CM mortality, morbidity, biomarker, and function hierarchy
summary: ATTRibute-CM adds a second TTR-stabilizer clinical-outcome signal in ATTR-CM, with a mechanism that prevents new misfolding rather than clearing deposits.
statement: In ATTR cardiomyopathy, acoramidis significantly favored placebo on a hierarchical outcome containing mortality, cardiovascular hospitalization, NT-proBNP, and 6-minute-walk distance components.
quantitative_note: The primary hierarchical analysis favored acoramidis with win ratio 1.8 (95% CI 1.4 to 2.2; P<0.001); death and cardiovascular hospitalization contributed 58% of pairwise comparisons.
caveats: Acoramidis stabilizes TTR and slows new amyloid formation rather than actively removing existing aggregates.
The hierarchical endpoint combines mortality, morbidity, biomarker, and functional components rather than isolating aggregate clearance.
